NAME: ArcaneArmorBalancedEnchant
DESC: This armor pushes back against anything that threatens to knock its wearer to the ground. The wearer gains a +4 bonus to CMD against grapple, trip and overrun combat maneuvers.

NAME: ArcaneArmorInvulnerabilityEnchant
DESC: This suit of armor grants the wearer DR 5/magic.

NAME: ArcaneArmorShadowEnchant
DESC: This armor blurs the wearer whenever they try to hide, while also dampening the sound around them, granting a +5 competence bonus on Stealth skill checks. The armor's armor check penalty still applies normally.

NAME: ArcaneArmorShadowGreaterEnchant
DESC: As shadow, except it grants a +15 competence bonus on Stealth skill checks.

NAME: AdamantineArmorHeavyEnchant
DESC: Adamantine is extremely strong and favored by weapon and armor smiths alike for its ability to cut through solid barriers with ease and endure heavy blows. Armor made from adamantine grants its wearer damage reduction of 1/— if it's light armor, 2/— if it's medium armor, and 3/— if it's heavy armor. Adamantine is so costly that weapons and armor made from it are always of masterwork quality; the masterwork cost is included in the prices given. Thus, adamantine weapons and ammunition have a +1 enhancement bonus on attack rolls, and the armor check penalty of adamantine armor is lessened by 1 compared to ordinary armor of its type. Items without metal parts cannot be made from adamantine. An arrow could be made of adamantine, but a quarterstaff could not.

NAME: AdamantineArmorLightEnchant
DESC: Adamantine is extremely strong and favored by weapon and armor smiths alike for its ability to cut through solid barriers with ease and endure heavy blows. Armor made from adamantine grants its wearer damage reduction of 1/— if it's light armor, 2/— if it's medium armor, and 3/— if it's heavy armor. Adamantine is so costly that weapons and armor made from it are always of masterwork quality; the masterwork cost is included in the prices given. Thus, adamantine weapons and ammunition have a +1 enhancement bonus on attack rolls, and the armor check penalty of adamantine armor is lessened by 1 compared to ordinary armor of its type. Items without metal parts cannot be made from adamantine. An arrow could be made of adamantine, but a quarterstaff could not.

NAME: AdamantineArmorMediumEnchant
DESC: Adamantine is extremely strong and favored by weapon and armor smiths alike for its ability to cut through solid barriers with ease and endure heavy blows. Armor made from adamantine grants its wearer damage reduction of 1/— if it's light armor, 2/— if it's medium armor, and 3/— if it's heavy armor. Adamantine is so costly that weapons and armor made from it are always of masterwork quality; the masterwork cost is included in the prices given. Thus, adamantine weapons and ammunition have a +1 enhancement bonus on attack rolls, and the armor check penalty of adamantine armor is lessened by 1 compared to ordinary armor of its type. Items without metal parts cannot be made from adamantine. An arrow could be made of adamantine, but a quarterstaff could not.

NAME: Fortification25Enchant
DESC: This suit of armor or shield produces a magical force that protects vital areas of the wearer more effectively. When an enemy confirms a critical hit or lands a sneak attack against the wearer, there is a chance that the critical hit or sneak attack is negated and damage is instead rolled normally. The chance is 25% for light fortification, 50% for moderate fortification, and 75% for heavy fortification.

NAME: Fortification50Enchant
DESC: This suit of armor or shield produces a magical force that protects vital areas of the wearer more effectively. When an enemy confirms a critical hit or lands a sneak attack against the wearer, there is a chance that the critical hit or sneak attack is negated and damage is instead rolled normally. The chance is 25% for light fortification, 50% for moderate fortification, and 75% for heavy fortification.

NAME: Fortification75Enchant
DESC: This suit of armor or shield produces a magical force that protects vital areas of the wearer more effectively. When an enemy confirms a critical hit or lands a sneak attack against the wearer, there is a chance that the critical hit or sneak attack is negated and damage is instead rolled normally. The chance is 25% for light fortification, 50% for moderate fortification, and 75% for heavy fortification.

NAME: GreaterShadow
DESC: This armor grants its wearer a +10 competence bonus to Stealth skill checks.

NAME: MithralArmorEnchant
DESC: Mithral is a very rare silvery, glistening metal that is lighter than steel but just as hard. When worked like steel, it becomes a wonderful material from which to create armor, and is occasionally used for other items as well. Most mithral armors are one category lighter than normal for purposes of movement and other limitations. Heavy armors are treated as medium, and medium armors are treated as light, but light armors are still treated as light. This decrease does not apply to proficiency in wearing the armor. Spell failure chances for armors and shields made from mithral are decreased by 10%, maximum Dexterity bonuses are increased by 2, and armor check penalties are decreased by 3 (to a minimum of 0). An item made from mithral weighs half as much as the same item made from other metals.

NAME: Agile
DESC: Agile weapons are unusually well balanced and responsive. A wielder with the Weapon Finesse feat can choose to apply her Dexterity modifier to damage rolls with an agile weapon in place of her Strength modifier (whichever is higher). This modifier to damage is not increased for two-handed weapons, but is still reduced for off-hand weapons.

NAME: Anarchic
DESC: An anarchic weapon is infused with the power of chaos. It makes the weapon chaotically aligned and thus bypasses the corresponding damage reduction. It deals an extra 2d6 points of damage against all creatures of lawful alignment.

NAME: Axiomatic
DESC: An axiomatic weapon is infused with lawful power. It makes the weapon law-aligned and thus bypasses the corresponding damage reduction. It deals an extra 2d6 points of damage against chaotic creatures.

NAME: BaneAberration
DESC: A bane weapon excels against certain foes. Against a designated foe, the weapon's enhancement bonus is +2 better than its actual bonus. It also deals an extra 2d6 points of damage against such foes.

NAME: BaneAnimal
DESC: A bane weapon excels against certain foes. Against a designated foe, the weapon's enhancement bonus is +2 better than its actual bonus. It also deals an extra 2d6 points of damage against such foes.

NAME: BaneHumanoidGiant2d6
DESC: Against a designated foe it deals an extra 2d6 points of damage.

NAME: Bleed
DESC: Whenever a bleed weapon lands a hit, it makes the target bleed for 1d6 HP per round. Bleed will continue until the target receives magic healing or leaves combat.

NAME: Brass
DESC: Incinerating weapon is sheathed in fire that deals an extra 2d6 points of fire damage on a successful hit.

NAME: Brass2d8
DESC: Flaming weapon is sheathed in fire that deals an extra 2d8 points of fire damage on a successful hit.

NAME: BrilliantEnergy
DESC: A brilliant energy weapon ignores nonliving matter. Armor and shield bonuses to AC (including any enhancement bonuses to that armor) do not count against it because the weapon passes through armor. (Dexterity, deflection, dodge, natural armor, and other such bonuses still apply.) A brilliant energy weapon cannot harm undead, constructs, or objects.

NAME: Corrosive
DESC: Corrosive weapon deals an extra 1d6 points of acid damage on a successful hit.

NAME: Corrosive2d6
DESC: Caustic weapon deals an extra 2d6 points of acid damage on a successful hit.

NAME: CorrosiveBurst
DESC: A corrosive burst weapon functions as a corrosive weapon that also explodes with acid upon striking a successful critical hit. The acid does not harm the wielder. In addition to the extra 1d6 acid damage from the corrosive ability, a corrosive burst weapon deals an extra 1d10 points of acid damage on a successful critical hit. If the weapon's critical multiplier is ×3, add an extra 2d10 points of acid damage instead, and if the multiplier is ×4, add an extra 3d10 points of acid damage.

NAME: Deteriorative
DESC: Deteriorative weapons a bane to all living things and deal 3d6 points of negative energy damage on a successful hit.

NAME: Disruption
DESC: A disruption weapon is the bane of all undead. Any undead creature struck in combat must succeed on a DC 14 Will save or be destroyed. A disruption weapon must be a bludgeoning melee weapon.

NAME: ElderBrass
DESC: This weapon functions like incinerating, except it bypasses any resistance or immunity to fire the target might have.

NAME: ElderCorrosive
DESC: This weapon functions like corrosive, except it bypasses any resistance or immunity to acid the target might have.

NAME: ElderCorrosive2d6
DESC: This weapon functions like caustic, except it bypasses any resistance or immunity to acid the target might have.

NAME: ElderCorrosiveBurst
DESC: This weapon functions like corrosive burst, except it bypasses any resistance or immunity to acid the target might have.

NAME: ElderFlaming
DESC: This weapon functions like flaming, except it bypasses any resistance or immunity to fire the target might have.

NAME: ElderFlamingBurst
DESC: This weapon functions like flaming burst, except it bypasses any resistance or immunity to fire the target might have.

NAME: ElderFrost
DESC: This weapon functions like frost, except it bypasses any resistance or immunity to cold the target might have.

NAME: ElderIce2d6
DESC: This weapon functions like freezing, except it bypasses any resistance or immunity to cold the target might have.

NAME: ElderIcyBurst
DESC: This weapon functions like icy burst, except it bypasses any resistance or immunity to cold the target might have.

NAME: ElderShock
DESC: This weapon functions like shock, except it bypasses any resistance or immunity to electricity the target might have.

NAME: ElderShock2d6
DESC: This weapon functions like greater shock, except it bypasses any resistance or immunity to electricity the target might have.

NAME: ElderShockingBurst
DESC: This weapon functions like shocking burst, except it bypasses any resistance or immunity to electricity the target might have.

NAME: Enhancement1
DESC: Attacks with this weapon get +1 enhancement bonus on both attack and damage rolls.

NAME: Enhancement2
DESC: Attacks with this weapon get +2 enhancement bonus on both attack and damage rolls.

NAME: Enhancement3
DESC: Attacks with this weapon get +3 enhancement bonus on both attack and damage rolls.

NAME: Enhancement4
DESC: Attacks with this weapon get +4 enhancement bonus on both attack and damage rolls.

NAME: Enhancement5
DESC: Attacks with this weapon get +5 enhancement bonus on both attack and damage rolls.

NAME: Enhancement6
DESC: Attacks with this weapon get a +6 enhancement bonus on both attack and damage rolls.

NAME: Flaming
DESC: Flaming weapon is sheathed in fire that deals an extra 1d6 points of fire damage on a successful hit.

NAME: FlamingBurst
DESC: A flaming burst weapon functions as a flaming weapon that also explodes with flame upon striking a successful critical hit. The fire does not harm the wielder. In addition to the extra 1d6 fire damage from the flaming ability, a flaming burst weapon deals an extra 1d10 points of fire damage on a successful critical hit. If the weapon's critical multiplier is ×3, add an extra 2d10 points of fire damage instead, and if the multiplier is ×4, add an extra 3d10 points of fire damage.

NAME: Frost
DESC: Frost weapon is sheathed in a terrible, icy cold that deals an extra 1d6 points of cold damage on a successful hit.

NAME: Frost2d8
DESC: Greater frost weapon is sheathed in a terrible, icy cold that deals an extra 2d8 points of cold damage on a successful hit.

NAME: Furious
DESC: A furious weapon serves as a focus for its wielder's anger. When the wielder is raging or under the effect of a rage spell, the weapon's enhancement bonus is +2 better than normal.

NAME: Furyborn
DESC: This special ability can only be placed on melee weapons. A furyborn weapon draws power from the anger and frustration the wielder feels when battling foes that refuse to die. Each time the wielder damages an opponent with the weapon, its enhancement bonus increases by +1 when making attacks against that opponent. Enhancement bonus can't be increased this way beyond the maximum value of +5. This extra enhancement bonus goes away if the opponent dies, the wielder uses the weapon to attack a different creature, or 1 hour passes.

NAME: GhostTouch
DESC: A ghost touch weapon deals damage normally against incorporeal creatures, regardless of its bonus. An incorporeal creature's 50% reduction in damage from corporeal sources does not apply to attacks made against it with ghost touch weapons.

NAME: Heartseeker
DESC: A heartseeker weapon ignores the miss chance for concealment against most living targets, though the attack must still target the proper space. This special ability does not apply against aberrations, oozes, plants, outsiders with the elemental subtype, or any creature specifically noted to lack a heart.

NAME: Holy
DESC: A holy weapon is imbued with holy power. This power makes the weapon good-aligned and thus bypasses the corresponding damage reduction. It deals an extra 2d6 points of damage against all creatures of evil alignment.

NAME: Ice2d6
DESC: Freezing weapon is sheathed in a terrible, icy cold that deals an extra 2d6 points of cold damage on a successful hit.

NAME: IcyBurst
DESC: An icy burst weapon functions as a frost weapon that also explodes with frost upon striking a successful critical hit. The frost does not harm the wielder. In addition to the extra damage from the frost ability, an icy burst weapon deals an extra 1d10 points of cold damage on a successful critical hit. If the weapon's critical multiplier is ×3, add an extra 2d10 points of cold damage instead, and if the multiplier is ×4, add an extra 3d10 points.

NAME: Keen
DESC: Ability doubles the threat range of a weapon. This benefit doesn't stack with any other effects that expand the threat range of a weapon.

NAME: MagicWeapon
DESC: This enchantment makes the weapon magic and thus bypasses the corresponding damage reduction.

NAME: Masterwork
DESC: A masterwork weapon is a finely crafted version of a normal weapon. Wielding it provides a +1 enhancement bonus on attack rolls.

NAME: Necrotic
DESC: Necrotic weapons are a bane to all living things. They deal additional 1d6 points of negative energy damage on a successful hit.

NAME: NullifyingEnchantment
DESC: This special ability can only be placed on melee weapons. A nullifying weapon suppresses a creature's spell resistance for a short amount of time. Anytime the weapon strikes a creature with spell resistance, the target's spell resistance is reduced by 1 for 1 minute. On a confirmed critical hit, its spell resistance is instead reduced by 2. Multiple hits from a nullifying weapon stack.

NAME: Oversized
DESC: Weapon is exceptionally large and deals more damage, but attacks with it suffer a penalty of –2 to attack rolls.

NAME: Radiant
DESC: Radiant weapon is imbued with life essence itself and deals an extra 1d6 points of positive energy damage on a successful hit.

NAME: Sacrificial
DESC: Against a helpless foe, the weapon's enhancement bonus is +2 better than its actual bonus.

NAME: Shock
DESC: Shock weapon is sheathed in crackling electricity that deals an extra 1d6 points of electricity damage on a successful hit.

NAME: Shock2d6
DESC: Improved shock weapon is sheathed in crackling electricity that deals an extra 2d6 points of electricity damage on a successful hit.

NAME: ShockingBurst
DESC: A shocking burst weapon functions as a shock weapon that explodes with electricity upon striking a successful critical hit. The electricity does not harm the wielder. In addition to the extra electricity damage from the shock ability, a shocking burst weapon deals an extra 1d10 points of electricity damage on a successful critical hit. If the weapon's critical multiplier is ×3, add an extra 2d10 points of electricity damage instead, and if the multiplier is ×4, add an extra 3d10 points.

NAME: Speed
DESC: When making a full attack, the wielder of a speed weapon may make one extra attack with it. The attack uses the wielder's full base attack bonus, plus any modifiers appropriate to the situation. (This benefit is not cumulative with similar effects, such as a haste spell.)

NAME: Thundering
DESC: A thundering weapon creates a cacophonous roar like thunder whenever it strikes a target, dealing an extra 1d6 points of sonic damage.

NAME: ThunderingBurst
DESC: A thundering burst weapon functions as a thundering weapon that explodes with sonic upon striking a successful critical hit. The sonic does not harm the wielder. In addition to the extra sonic damage from the thundering ability, a thundering burst weapon deals an extra 1d10 points of sonic damage on a successful critical hit. If the weapon's critical multiplier is ×3, add an extra 2d10 points of sonic damage instead, and if the multiplier is ×4, add an extra 3d10 points.

NAME: Ultrasound
DESC: An ultrasound weapon creates a hardly hearable noise whenever it strikes a target, dealing an extra 2d6 points of sonic damage.

NAME: Unholy
DESC: An unholy weapon is imbued with unholy power. This power makes the weapon evil-aligned and thus bypasses the corresponding damage reduction. It deals an extra 2d6 points of damage against all creatures of good alignment.

NAME: ViciousEnchantment
DESC: When a vicious weapon strikes an opponent, it creates a flash of disruptive energy that resonates between the opponent and the wielder. This energy deals an extra 2d6 points of damage to the opponent and 1d6 points of damage to the wielder.

NAME: Vorpal
DESC: This potent and feared ability allows the weapon to sever the heads of those it strikes. Upon a roll of natural 20 (followed by a successful roll to confirm the critical hit), the weapon severs the opponent's head (if it has one) from its body. Some creatures, such as many aberrations and all oozes, have no heads. Others, such as golems and undead creatures other than vampires, are not affected by the loss of their heads. Most other creatures, however, die when their heads are cut off.

NAME: DisruptingWeapon
DESC: A disruption weapon is the bane of all undead. Any undead creature struck in combat must succeed on a DC 14 Will save or be destroyed. A disruption weapon must be a bludgeoning melee weapon.

NAME: DragonEssenceBaneDragon
DESC: A bane weapon excels against certain foes. Against a designated foe, the weapon's enhancement bonus is +2 better than its actual bonus. It also deals an extra 2d6 points of damage against such foes.

NAME: MagesDisjunctionEnchantment
DESC: Magical effects of this item are suppressed.

NAME: TwoHandedDamageMultiplierEnchantment
DESC: Attacks are made with a 1.5 damage multipler.

NAME: SteelHeadbuttEnchant
DESC: Weapon uses armor enchants and material.
